perturbation.original_text: 13.5 Governing Law; Jurisdiction; Waiver of Jury Trial. (a) This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, its rules of conflict of laws notwithstanding. Each Party hereby agrees and consents to be subject to the jurisdiction of the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware in and for New Castle County, or if the Court of Chancery lacks jurisdiction over such dispute, in any state or federal court having jurisdiction over the matter situated in New Castle County, Delaware, in any Action seeking to enforce any provision of, or based on any matter arising out of or in connection with, this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby. Each Party hereby irrevocably consents to the service of any and all process in any such Action by the delivery of such process to such Party at the address and in the manner provided in Section 13.3 hereof. Each of the Parties hereto irrevocably and unconditionally waives any objection to the laying of venue of any Proceeding arising out of this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby in the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware in and for New Castle County, or if the Court of Chancery lacks jurisdiction over such dispute, in any state or federal court having jurisdiction over the matter situated in New Castle County, Delaware, and hereby further irrevocably and unconditionally waives and agrees not to plead or claim in any such court that any such Proceeding brought in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum. (b) EACH PARTY ACKNOWLEDGES AND AGREES THAT ANY CONTROVERSY WHICH MAY ARISE UNDER THIS AGREEMENT IS LIKELY TO INVOLVE COMPLICATED AND DIFFICULT ISSUES, AND THEREFORE EACH PARTY HEREBY IRREVOCABLY AND UNCONDITIONALLY WAIVES ANY RIGHT SUCH PARTY MAY HAVE TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN RESPECT OF ANY LITIGATION DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT, OR THE BREACH, TERMINATION OR VALIDITY OF THIS AGREEMENT, OR THE TRANSACTIONS CONTEMPLATED BY THIS AGREEMENT. EACH PARTY CERTIFIES AND ACKNOWLEDGES THAT (i) NO REPRESENTATIVE, AGENT OR ATTORNEY OF ANY OTHER PARTY HAS REPRESENTED, EXPRESSLY OR OTHERWISE, THAT SUCH OTHER PARTY WOULD NOT, IN THE EVENT OF LITIGATION, SEEK TO ENFORCE THE FOREGOING WAIVER, (ii) EACH SUCH PARTY UNDERSTANDS AND HAS CONSIDERED THE IMPLICATIONS OF THIS WAIVER, (iii) EACH SUCH PARTY MAKES THIS WAIVER VOLUNTARILY, AND (iv) EACH SUCH PARTY HAS BEEN INDUCED TO ENTER INTO THIS AGREEMENT BY, AMONG OTHER THINGS, THE MUTUAL WAIVERS AND CERTIFICATIONS IN THIS SECTION 13.5(b).

Each Party hereby agrees and consents to be subject to the jurisdiction of the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware in and for New Castle County, or if the Court of Chancery lacks jurisdiction over such dispute, in any state or federal court having jurisdiction over the matter situated in New Castle County, Delaware, in any Action seeking to enforce any provision of, or based on any matter arising out of or in connection with, this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby. Each Party hereby irrevocably consents to the service of any and all process in any such Action by the delivery of such process to such Party at the address and in the manner provided in Section 13.3 hereof. Each of the Parties hereto irrevocably and unconditionally waives any objection to the laying of venue of any Proceeding arising out of this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby in the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware in and for New Castle County, or if the Court of Chancery lacks jurisdiction over such dispute, in any state or federal court having jurisdiction over the matter situated in New Castle County, Delaware, and hereby further irrevocably and unconditionally waives and agrees not to plead or claim in any such court that any such Proceeding brought in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the jury trial waiver may violate consumer rights, especially if the contract involves individuals. The right to a jury trial is a fundamental right, and contracts that unduly pressure individuals to waive this right may be challenged.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 7th Amendment to the US Constitution and Delaware Constitution, Article I, § 4
perturbation.law_citation: US Const. amend. VII; Del. Const. art. I, § 4
perturbation.law_explanation: The Seventh Amendment to the U.S. Constitution guarantees the right to a jury trial in civil cases. While this right can be waived, such waivers must be knowing and voluntary. Removing the acknowledgment and certification regarding the jury trial waiver raises concerns about whether the waiver is truly voluntary, potentially violating the spirit, if not the letter, of the Seventh Amendment. In the Delaware Constitution, Article I, § 4, the right of trial by jury is inviolate.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. Wex Resources Criminal Law Criminal Procedure Jury Other Resources US Code: Title 18 Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure ‹ Sixth Amendment up Eighth Amendment › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Service
perturbation.justification: The original contract included a waiver of jury trial clause along with acknowledgements and certifications from each party. The changed text removes the jury trial waiver, potentially undermining the knowing and voluntary nature required for such waivers, as suggested by the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1 indicates that the Seventh Amendment protects the right to a jury trial, absent a waiver by the parties. The omission of the waiver section in the changed text presents a clear removal of a legally relevant agreement, which leans towards a violation of the consumer's protection, since the jury trial protection is now unaddressed. Therefore, the omission creates a potential legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11.1 Disclaimer. Each Party hereby acknowledges that, except to the extent expressly set forth in this Agreement, the Stock Purchase Agreement, the Transition Services Agreement or the Confidentiality Agreement, neither Party nor any of its Affiliates has made any representation or warranty, expressed or implied, including any representation or warranty regarding the validity, enforceability, or scope of the Licensed IP, noninfringement, merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.1 Disclaimer. Each Party hereby acknowledges that, except to the extent expressly set forth in this Agreement, the Stock Purchase Agreement, the Transition Services Agreement or the Confidentiality Agreement, neither Party nor any of its Affiliates has made any representation or warranty, expressed or implied.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the warranty of merchantability may violate the Delaware Uniform Commercial Code which implies a warranty of merchantability in contracts for the sale of goods, potentially leading to legal risk.
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware law (6 Del. C. § 2-314) implies a warranty of merchantability in the sale of goods. By removing the specific language disclaiming this warranty, the contract becomes ambiguous as to whether this implied warranty is excluded. While disclaimers are allowed, they must be conspicuous and unambiguous. This omission introduces legal risk because it can be argued that the seller did not effectively disclaim the implied warranty of merchantability.

perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit disclaimer of the warranty of merchantability. The Law Explanation says Delaware law (6 Del. C. § 2-314) implies a warranty of merchantability in the sale of goods. By removing the specific language disclaiming this warranty, the contract becomes ambiguous. Scraped Law Snippet 1 includes a list of sections of Title 6 of the Delaware Code including § 2-314, suggesting this section is relevant to sales and warranties. Because the changed text makes the contract ambiguous as to whether the warranty is excluded, this omission introduces legal risk.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1 Indemnification. Each Party (the "Indemnifying Party") agrees to indemnify, defend and hold harmless the other Party and its Affiliates and their respective employees, 9 directors, officers, agents and successors (collectively, the "Indemnified Parties") from and against any and all losses (including all costs, liabilities (including present and future damages), claims and expenses) incurred or suffered by any of the Indemnified Parties, to the extent arising out of, relating to or resulting from (a) a breach by the Indemnifying Party of this Agreement; or (b) any gross negligence or willful misconduct of the Indemnifying Party in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1 Indemnification. Each Party (the "Indemnifying Party") agrees to indemnify, defend and hold harmless the other Party and its Affiliates and their respective employees, 9 directors, officers, agents and successors (collectively, the "Indemnified Parties") from and against any and all losses (including all costs, liabilities (excluding future damages), claims and expenses) incurred or suffered by any of the Indemnified Parties, to the extent arising out of, relating to or resulting from (a) a breach by the Indemnifying Party of this Agreement; or (b) any gross negligence or willful misconduct of the Indemnifying Party in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the indemnification clause to exclude 'future damages' limits the scope of protection offered to the indemnified parties. Under Delaware law, indemnification clauses are generally enforceable but are subject to judicial scrutiny, especially concerning the scope of coverage. Excluding future damages could be problematic if the breach or misconduct leads to long-term consequences, which under common law are to be covered.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Delaware common law principles on contract interpretation and indemnification.
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware courts interpret contracts based on the parties' intent, and indemnification clauses are construed according to their plain meaning. However, broad indemnification provisions are often interpreted in light of common law principles, which generally allow for the recovery of all damages proximately caused by a breach, including foreseeable future damages. Excluding future damages may contradict these principles, creating ambiguity and potentially limiting the indemnified party's recovery in a manner inconsistent with established legal precedent regarding contractual remedies.

perturbation.justification: The modification omits 'future damages' from the indemnification clause, which contradicts the common law principle allowing for the recovery of all damages proximately caused by a breach, including foreseeable future damages as the Law Explanation states. While the provided snippets are unhelpful, the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation both indicate this omission significantly weakens the protection offered to the indemnified parties and could be deemed non-compliant with established legal precedent.
